container.title: Part B. Breadth studies
First year engineering breadth studies
container.description:
You must complete 6 credit points (one unit) from the following list.

Note 1: If you are required to complete one, or both, of the foundational studies units, you are not required to complete 6 credit points of First year engineering breadth studies.

Note 2. Biomedical engineering: If you intend to specialise in biomedical engineering, you must select and complete BMS1021.

Note 3. Chemical engineering: Due to the prohibition with CHE2161, you are strongly advised against completing MEC2404 as a First Year elective. This choice may result in an inadequate foundation for the subsequent core unit CHE3167 in the chemical engineering specialisation.

Note 4. Software engineering: If you intend to specialise in software engineering, you must select and complete FIT1045, as it provides essential grounding in programming and prepares you for advanced software engineering units.

Note 5. CHM1011 and CHM1051 are mutual prohibition units, meaning that you can only complete either CHM1011 or CHM1051.

Note 6. If you complete a First Year engineering breadth unit that happens to be a core unit in your chosen specialisation, the unit will count towards your specialisation study. You must still fulfil the First Year engineering breadth study requirement by completing another breadth study unit

container.title: Foundational studies
container.description:
If you have not completed either or both VCE Physics (Units 3 and 4) and VCE Specialist mathematics (Units 3 and 4) or equivalent and with the required study score, you must complete the following physics and/or mathematics foundation units.

To check the required study score, visit https://www.monash.edu/engineering/current-students/enrolment-and-re-enrolment/how-to-enrol-and-re-enrol/course-information/undergraduate-enrolment

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: Foundational studies
Curriculum Structure Container: Foundational studies
Curriculum Structure Container: Part B. Breadth studies

container.description:
You must complete 6 credit points (one unit) from either Foundational studies or First year engineering breadth studies.

Note 1: If you have not completed either or both VCE Physics (Units 3 and 4) and VCE Specialist mathematics (Units 3 and 4) or equivalent and with the required study score, you must complete the related foundational studies unit(s) in place of the First year engineering breadth studies.

Note 2: If you require both foundation units, the second foundation unit will increase your total credit points needed for the double degree by 6 credit points. A study overload will occur in Year 1 or 2.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: Engineering component

container.description:
You must complete 144 credit points comprising all of Part A, six credit points from Part B, all of Part C and all of Part D as described in the Bachelor of Engineering (Honours).

The Common First Year consists of Parts A and B.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure: 2026.01.133

Discipline elective studies
container.description: You must complete 24 credit points of discipline elective studies  which can be combined into a minor of study different to the chosen major from Part A and with no more than 12 credit points at level 1.

container.title: Professional Futures
Global immersion
container.description:
You may complete the following units or any of the Arts study tours or semester length programs by submitting an enquiry to Monash Connect. Please note, study tours must be offered by the Faculty of Arts in order to be counted as Professional Futures units.

Please visit https://www.monash.edu/arts/learning-abroad/global-study-tours for more information.
